subject:
Combining WARP projects, acquired on different days with different
 gainReferences?
body:
hi folks,

   1. do you ever combine your WARP projects, acquired on different days, 
   with possibly quite different gain references into a single WARP project 
   folder?
   2. The logs in the command prompt during subtomo creation suggest that 
   WARP loads gainReference when creating subtomos. I am not sure if it 
   actually loads it. If it does, then does it load from the gainPath in 
   .options file or .xml files. I notice that .xml files may not even store 
   gainPath in many cases. Maybe WARP is able to create subtomos from 
   ‘averaged’ frames in the averages folder. E.g., I seem to remember that 
   renaming the average folder in the WARP project prevents subtomo 
   generation, so maybe WARP does use these gain-corrected ‘averaged’ frames 
   for subtomo generation.
   3. So the question is does WARP load and need gainReference when 
   creating subtomos? If yes, then does it load the same gainReference for all 
   subtomos or can figure out the right gain reference for each micrographName 
   or tomostar of different projects, with different gainReferences when 
   projects are combined?
   4. I like to keep separate WARP projects for separate microscopy 
   sessions and then create subtomos for them separately and then I combine 
   their star files for refinement in Relion. This works well for Relion, but 
   to run M, I think it would be best if a single M project, combining all 
   tomostars, can be run from a single project directory with all tomostars 
   combined.
   5. To test if combining projects works well; I did combine projects and 
   it works well. But I want to doubly make sure that combining and then 
   creating subtomos from the combined project folder is fine.
   

Thanks and cheers,
Digvijay
